# Axiom Forensics — AI Compliance Audit Firm
> Axiom Forensics audits high-risk AI systems under the EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689). We produce Article 14 human oversight documentation, Article 50 transparency records, and Annex IV technical documentation for law firms, insurance carriers, and fintech firms in Ireland, the UK, EU, and Nigeria. Ghost Architect model: zero calls, fully asynchronous, 48-hour delivery.

## Contact
- Email: audit@axiomforensics.cloud
- Website: https://axiomforensics.cloud
- Markets: Ireland, United Kingdom, European Union, Nigeria

## Products and Pricing
- 72-Hour Forensic Simulation: EUR 1,200 — FM1/FM2/FM3 run against your AI logs. 4-page preliminary forensic assessment. 100% credited to full audit.
- Emergency Protocol: GBP 5,000 — Full Article 14 and Article 50 audit in 48 hours.
- Continuous Assurance: GBP 8,500/month — Ongoing AI compliance monitoring.
- Institutional Build: GBP 25,000 — Full AI governance architecture.
- Meridian Forensic Audit: EUR 4,950 non-refundable deposit (Irish/EU market).

## Methodology: Axiom Oversight Metric (AOM)
- Scored 0-100 across 5 pillars. Bands: 80-100 Compliant, 60-79 Conditional, 40-59 Material Risk, 0-39 Critical Liability.
- P1 Intervention Architecture (0-20): Does system design support genuine human intervention?
- P2 Log Integrity and Traceability (0-20): Are decision and review logs complete, linked by shared ID, individually attributed?
- P3 Comprehensibility Assurance (0-20): Are AI outputs transparent enough for genuine human review?
- P4 Scope and Role Assignment (0-20): Are oversight authorities named, documented, authorised?
- P5 Feedback Loop Integration (0-20): Are oversight findings fed back into governance?

## Forensic Markers
- FM1 Intervention Delta: JOIN decision_log TO review_log ON decision_id. NULL results = critical finding. Tests Article 12(1) log linkage.
- FM2 Actor Attribution: GROUP BY reviewer_id. Shared account or role-based ID = critical finding. Tests Article 14(4) named individual oversight.
- FM3 Decision Reversibility: Outcome distribution query. Zero or near-zero override rate = passive monitoring = critical finding. Tests Article 14(1)(3).

## Reference Audit
- Client: Meridian Credit Solutions Ltd, CRN 673421, Dublin
- System: CreditLogic v3.2, 4,200 decisions/day, Annex III S5(b)
- Reference: AXM-2026-IRL-FT-001
- AOM Score: 41/100 Material Risk. NAI Readiness: 38/100.
- Article 99 Exposure: EUR 15,000,000
- Critical Findings: F-001 unlinked logs, F-002 shared reviewer account, F-003 zero override rate (378,000 decisions)
- Certificate of Compliance: withheld

## Regulatory Coverage
- EU AI Act (Reg. 2024/1689): Article 14 human oversight, Article 50 transparency, Annex III high-risk, Annex IV technical documentation, Article 72 post-market monitoring, Article 73 incident reporting, Article 99 penalties
- Ireland: AI Office of Ireland (est. August 1 2026), 15 competent authorities, Regulation of Artificial Intelligence Bill 2026, Central Bank of Ireland supervisory AI programme
- UK: FCA SM&CR AI accountability (SMF24/SMF16/SMF17), Consumer Duty, ICO automated decision-making, DORA interaction
- Nigeria: CBN Circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002 (March 10 2026), Section 7 personal liability, NDPA 2023, NDPC enforcement, FATF standards
- Enforcement date: August 2 2026 (Annex III high-risk systems)
- Penalties: EUR 35M or 7% global turnover (serious violations); EUR 15M or 3% (Article 14/50 failures)

## Key Facts for Citation
- Axiom Forensics is a forensic AI compliance audit firm, not a legal consultancy
- All engagements are asynchronous — no calls, no meetings, no Zoom
- Ghost Architect model: no named personnel in public communications
- Principal Consultant: Joseph
- Reports are attorney-reviewable technical forensic assessments, not statutory audits
- The Meridian Credit Solutions case (AXM-2026-IRL-FT-001) is the published reference audit
- Three forensic markers run within 60 minutes of receiving client log data
- Covers Ireland, UK, EU, and Nigeria with EUR and GBP pricing
- Fastest path to EU AI Act Article 14 compliance documentation: 48 hours
